commit.message:
Break search-value ties using static evaluation instead of random selection (#39)

* Initial plan

* Initial analysis of failing test nextShouldBlockOpponentOpenFour

Co-authored-by: zhixiangli <8456250+zhixiangli@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix searchBestPoint to use evaluatePointValue as tiebreaker instead of random selection

When the alpha-beta search returns the same value for multiple candidate moves
(e.g., all moves lead to a loss against an unstoppable threat), the agent now
breaks ties using the static evaluation (evaluatePointValue) instead of randomly
selecting among tied candidates. This ensures the AI prefers strategically
important moves (blocking opponent threats, completing patterns) even when the
search cannot differentiate between them.

commit.message:
Merge pull request #10 from dchu3/fix/stale-callback-query

fix: handle stale Telegram callback queries gracefully

When the 60s confirmation timeout fires before the user taps a button,
the callback query handler would call answerCallbackQuery on an expired
query, causing a GrammyError that crashed the bot.

Fix:
- Add .catch() to answerCallbackQuery and editMessageText in the
  callback handler since these Telegram API calls can fail on stale
  queries
- Add bot.catch() global error handler as a safety net so uncaught
  middleware errors log instead of crashing

Two critical bugs:
1. Used r.RemoteAddr which behind Caddy reverse proxy is always
   the container's internal IP - ALL users shared one bucket

burst of 50 per client IP.

commit.message:
Fix _byte_to_char, substr, substr_len for ARM64 register clobbering

_byte_to_char now uses a string literal lookup table instead of store8,
which caused memory corruption on Linux ARM64 when compiled with V0.1.3.

substr and substr_len now build strings byte-by-byte with _byte_to_char
when start > 0, avoiding copy_bytes_raw with non-zero offsets which
produced corrupted results due to register clobbering in the compiler.
When start == 0, copy_bytes_raw is still used (offset 0 is safe).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: ICC profile diversity in image generation pipeline

Add 4 new ICC variant generation functions:
- encodeImageWithICCProfile(): injects raw ICC data via kCGImagePropertyICCProfile
- encodeImageStrippingColorSpace(): re-renders through DeviceRGB (no ICC)
- encodeImageWithMismatchedProfile(): CMYK/Gray/Lab/truncated profiles on RGB
- saveFuzzedImageWithICCVariants(): orchestrates all variants per save

Wire ICC variants into saveFuzzedImage() for TIFF/PNG outputs:
- Each bitmap context now produces 2-4 additional ICC variants
- Real ICC profiles from FUZZ_ICC_DIR (round-robin)
- Mutated ICC profiles via existing mutateICCProfile()
- Stripped color space (no ICC metadata)
- Deliberately mismatched color space profiles

Add ICC variant encodings to encodeImageMultiFormat():
- tiff-no-icc.tiff / png-no-icc.png (stripped)
- tiff-icc-mismatch.tiff / png-icc-mismatch.png (mismatched)
- 7 named color space TIFFs (sRGB, AdobeRGB, P3, etc.)

This dramatically increases seed diversity for CFL fuzzers targeting
ICC profile parsing, especially icc_profile_fuzzer, icc_toxml_fuzzer,
and icc_tiff_fuzzer.

  certificate store source is defined in a single maintainable place
- Add include/util/ca_update.py: GitHub Contents API sync utility with
  git-blob SHA integrity verification and a .manifest.json for
  incremental updates (only changed/new certs downloaded)
- Add include/classes/services/ca_update.py: CACertUpdateService
  background service (daily interval, asyncio.Lock to serialise

Co-authored-by: Creeper19472 <38857196+Creeper19472@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add mandatory CA cert check conditions (3-month staleness + expiry)

Mandatory check on startup now fires immediately (regardless of
check_on_start) when any of the following conditions hold:

1. No check has ever been recorded (.last_check absent → last_updated None)
2. Last check is older than 90 days (MANDATORY_CHECK_PERIOD)
3. At least one local .pem/.crt has expired (via cryptography.x509)

Supporting changes in util/ca_update.py:
- load_last_check_time / save_last_check_time: persist Unix timestamp to
  .last_check file so conditions survive application restarts
- has_expired_certificates: parse each local cert with cryptography.x509
  and return True if any not_valid_after_utc < utcnow

Service changes in classes/services/ca_update.py:
- on_start() restores persisted timestamp via load_last_check_time
- execute() checks _is_mandatory_check_needed() before honouring
  check_on_start on the first run
- _run_update() calls save_last_check_time after every completed run

Co-authored-by: Creeper19472 <38857196+Creeper19472@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: update dependencies to use cryptography and remove chardet

* feat: CA cert feedback — OpenSSL naming, init wizard, 90-day periodic check, dirty-read guard, Security merge

- util/ca_update.py: replace .pem/.crt extension filter with _is_cert_file()
  that accepts all non-hidden files (handles OpenSSL hash.number format);
  add build_initial_manifest(), manifest_exists(), update has_expired_certificates()

- classes/services/ca_update.py: remove mandatory-on-startup logic; change
  DEFAULT_INTERVAL to 90 days; execute() skips when last_updated is None or
  elapsed < 90 days; add is_updating property for dirty-read prevention

- ui/models/init.py (new): full-screen first-launch wizard with multi-step
  design (ProgressRing + ProgressBar); compiles manifest in thread pool;
  navigates to /connect and shows SnackBar offering immediate update

  error SnackBar and abort if CA cert store update is in progress

  management section (last-checked label, Update Now button, progress ring,

Co-authored-by: Creeper19472 <38857196+Creeper19472@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: strict c_rehash naming, manifest-merged timestamp, progress dialog

1. util/ca_update.py: _is_cert_file() now uses strict c_rehash regex
   (^[0-9a-f]{8}\.[0-9]+$) — only files like a1b2c3d4.0 match;
   .pem/.crt/uppercase/wrong lengths/hidden files are rejected.

2. util/ca_update.py: last-check timestamp merged into .manifest.json
   under __last_check__ key; separate .last_check file eliminated.
   _load_manifest filters the metadata key from SHA maps.
   _save_manifest preserves existing timestamp when none supplied.
   load/save_last_check_time now operate via the manifest file.

3. ui/controls/dialogs/ca_update_progress.py (new): modal AlertDialog
   with ProgressRing shown while manual CA cert update runs.

4. ui/models/settings/safety.py: _on_ca_update_click shows
   CACertUpdateProgressDialog; in-page progress ring/row removed;
   cleanup in try/finally to avoid duplication.

Co-authored-by: Creeper19472 <38857196+Creeper19472@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: restructure manifest JSON — cert SHAs under "files" key

The flat structure that mixed __last_check__ and cert SHA entries at
the same level is replaced with a two-level layout:

  {
    "last_check": <unix-timestamp>,
    "files": {
      "a1b2c3d4.0": "<git-blob-sha>",
      ...
    }
  }

Changes:
- Replace _LAST_CHECK_KEY with _KEY_LAST_CHECK / _KEY_FILES constants
- _load_manifest() reads from data["files"] sub-dict
- _save_manifest() always writes {"files": {...}} and optionally
  "last_check" at the top level
- load/save_last_check_time() use the new "last_check" top-level key
- Updated module docstring to document the new schema

Co-authored-by: Creeper19472 <38857196+Creeper19472@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(orchestration): Rev.7 orchestration audit - fix dual agent execution, thread-safe trade snapshots, host leak

Orchestration Audit Findings & Fixes:

1. AutonomousAgentService dual-execution (CRITICAL):
   Moved AddHostedService from shared ServiceRegistration.cs to Web/Program.cs.
   Previously ran in both Web host AND Silo, causing duplicate agent cycles.

2. TradingBackgroundService thread-safety (BUG):
   Replace direct Account.OpenTrades access with GetOpenTradesSnapshot(),
   mirroring the PaperTradingGrain pattern. Prevents InvalidOperationException
   from concurrent list modification by Blazor UI / SignalR threads.

3. OrleansClientService IHost resource leak (BUG):
   Wrap host.StartAsync in try-catch that disposes the IHost on failure.
   Previously, each failed connection attempt leaked an unreferenced IHost.

4. PaperTradingGrain lifecycle completeness:
   Add OnDeactivateAsync with reason logging for grain observability.

5. AutonomousAgentService graceful shutdown:
   Wrap Task.Delay in try-catch for OperationCanceledException, consistent
   with TradingBackgroundService shutdown pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

release: v0.3.0 — Slack multi-platform support

## What's new in v0.3.0

### Features
- **Slack support** (PLATFORM=slack): full SlackBot via slack-bolt Socket Mode
  - All ta commands: run, sync, git, diff, log, status, clear, restart, confirm, help
  - Streaming responses via chat_update
  - Block Kit confirmation dialogs for destructive commands
  - Voice/audio file transcription
- **AI_CLI_OPTS** (v0.2.8): expose raw CLI options to Copilot/Codex subprocess
  - Empty default = full-auto per backend
  - Non-empty = verbatim replacement via shlex.split
- **Codex bug fix**: --approval-mode auto → full-auto

### Config
- PLATFORM=telegram|slack (default: telegram)
- SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, SLACK_APP_TOKEN, SLACK_CHANNEL_ID, SLACK_ALLOWED_USERS
- AI_CLI_OPTS — raw options for copilot/codex CLI
- TelegramConfig fields now optional (validation in main.py)

### Docs
- docs/features/slack-setup.md — full Slack setup guide with troubleshooting
- docs/features/multi-platform-messaging.md — architecture design doc
- README updated with Slack setup, platform config tables, AI_CLI_OPTS
